definitions.definition: Electromagnetic waves are waves that are created as a result of vibrations between an electric field and a magnetic field. They can propagate through a vacuum.
definitions.example: Radio waves, microwaves, infrared waves, visible light, ultraviolet rays, X-rays, and gamma rays are all examples of electromagnetic waves.

definitions.definition: The region around a charged particle in which the force of electrostatic attraction or repulsion can be detected. The direction of an electric field is from the positive charge to the negative charge.
definitions.example: The electric field around a stationary charge is radial and depends on the magnitude of the charge and the distance from the charge.

definitions.definition: A region around a magnet or a current-carrying conductor in which the force of magnetism can be detected. The direction of a magnetic field is from the north pole to the south pole.
definitions.example: The magnetic field around a current-carrying wire is circular and depends on the magnitude of the current and the distance from the wire.

definitions.definition: A wave in which the displacement of the medium is perpendicular to the direction of propagation of the wave.
definitions.example: Electromagnetic waves, such as light waves, are transverse waves.

definitions.definition: A wave in which the displacement of the medium is parallel to the direction of propagation of the wave.
The speed of propagation of electromagnetic waves in free space. It is a universal constant and is denoted by the symbol c.
definitions.example: The speed of light in free space is approximately 3 x 10^8 m/s.

definitions.definition: The distance between two consecutive points on a wave that are in phase with each other. It is denoted by the symbol λ.
definitions.example: The wavelength of a wave is measured in meters.

definitions.definition: The number of oscillations or cycles of a wave per second. It is denoted by the symbol ν.
definitions.example: The frequency of a wave is measured in hertz (Hz).

definitions.definition: The time taken by a wave to complete one oscillation or cycle. It is denoted by the symbol T.
definitions.example: The period of a wave is measured in seconds.

definitions.definition: The maximum displacement of a wave from its equilibrium position. It is denoted by the symbol A.
definitions.example: The amplitude of a wave is measured in meters.

definitions.definition: The amount of energy transmitted per unit area per unit time. It is denoted by the symbol I.
definitions.example: The intensity of a wave is measured in watts per square meter.

definitions.definition: A range of frequencies of electromagnetic waves, extending from low-frequency waves such as radio waves to high-frequency waves such as gamma rays.
definitions.example: The electromagnetic spectrum includes radio waves, microwaves, infrared waves, visible light, ultraviolet rays, X-rays, and gamma rays.

definitions.definition: The orientation of the electric field vector of an electromagnetic wave.
definitions.example: Linear polarization, circular polarization, and elliptical polarization are examples of polarization.

definitions.definition: The change in direction of an electromagnetic wave when it hits a surface and bounces back.
definitions.example: The reflection of light from a mirror is an example of reflection.

definitions.definition: The change in direction of an electromagnetic wave when it passes from one medium to another.
definitions.example: The refraction of light from air into a glass prism is an example of refraction.

definitions.definition: The bending of an electromagnetic wave around an obstacle or through a narrow slit.
definitions.example: The diffraction of light from a single slit is an example of diffraction.

definitions.definition: The superposition of two or more electromagnetic waves resulting in a new wave pattern.
definitions.example: The interference of light from two slits is an example of interference.

definitions.definition: The superposition of two electromagnetic waves resulting in a wave with greater amplitude.
definitions.example: The constructive interference of light from two slits results in a bright fringe.

definitions.definition: The superposition of two electromagnetic waves resulting in a wave with lesser amplitude.
definitions.example: The destructive interference of light from two slits results in a dark fringe.
